feed.title: Scholar Alerts — Clean Feed
feed.subtitle: Google Scholar alerts reformatted as individual article entries

entry.title: Observing the Alpha Cen Ab Planet Candidate Using the Roman Space Telescope Coronagraph
entry.content:
Authors: E Bendek, R Belikov, D Sirbu, D Mawet, C Beichman... - ... Society Meeting Abstracts, 2026

Abstract: Abstract The Roman Space Telescope Coronagraph offers a unique opportunity to observe our nearest solar twin, Alpha Centauri A, and characterize the habitable- zone gas giant candidate, Alpha Cen Ab. The candidate uniquely stands out among ...

Tags: Reviewer, Too: Exoplanets

Alert: Renyu Hu (author alert)

entry.title: How compactness curbs entanglement growth in bosonic systems
entry.content:
Authors: S Aimet, P Schmoll, J Eisert, J Schmiedmayer... - arXiv preprint arXiv ..., 2026

Abstract: Zero modes, understood here as degrees of freedom with vanishing confining frequency, play a central role in the nonequilibrium dynamics of bosonic systems. In Gaussian models, however, they lead to an unbounded, logarithmic growth of ...

Alert: Jens Eisert (author alert)

entry.title: ucdmcmc: A Fast Fitting Algorithm for Cool Star, Brown Dwarf, and Exoplanet Atmospheres
entry.content:
Authors: A Burgasser, H Brooks, J Haynes, S Morrissey... - ... Society Meeting Abstracts, 2026

Abstract: We present ucdmcmc, a simple and speedy algorithm for fitting low-temperature atmosphere models to the spectra of low-mass stars, brown dwarfs, and extrasolar planets. ucdmcmc enables fits to over 20 precomputed grid models, and combines ...

Tags: Reviewer, Too: Exoplanets

Alert: Adam J. Burgasser (author alert)

entry.title: GEMS JWST: A sub-Solar metallicity atmosphere for giant planet TOI-5293Ab orbiting a rapidly changing M-dwarf
entry.content:
Authors: S Kanodia, CI Cañas, J Lustig-Yaeger, GG Caloca... - arXiv preprint arXiv ..., 2026

Abstract: The growing sample of Giant Exoplanets around M-dwarf Stars (GEMS) helps probe the extremes of giant planet formation. Comparing the properties of this sample with their FGK counterparts can help us understand how planet formation and migration ...

Tags: Reviewer, Too: Astrobiology, Reviewer, Too: Exoplanets

Alert: Jacob Lustig-Yaeger (author alert)

entry.title: Kinematic Emergence of the Page Curve in a Local Transverse-Field Ising Model
entry.content:
Authors: SJW Jones, M Altaie, BTH Varcoe -arXivpreprintarXiv:2603.17000, 2026

Abstract: We present a controllable quantum spin-chain model that reproduces the Page curve (the rise-and-fall of bipartite entanglement expected in black-hole evaporation), using only local interactions and a kinematic reduction of the subsystem size. Two ...

Alert: arxiv" + intitle:"emergence (keyword alert)

entry.title: Exo-Earths from the Inside Out: Characterizing Earth-like Exoplanets with Future Space Telescopes
entry.content:
Authors: S Gilbert-Janizek, R Barnes, J Lustig-Yaeger, P Driscoll... - ... Society Meeting Abstracts, 2026

Abstract: ... Future space telescopes will seek extraterrestrial life on Earth-like planets via transmission and direct ... exoplanets around FGK stars identified as high-priority HWO targets. We find that planets receiving Earth-like instellation around K-dwarfs ...

Tags: Reviewer, Too: Astrobiology, Reviewer, Too: Exoplanets

entry.title: Synbiotics in dog-feeding studies

author.name: origins of life"|"origin of life"|"emergence of ...

entry.content:
Authors: AC Beynen

Abstract: ... (Note 1): combination of a probiotic (beneficial bacterium) and prebiotic (fiber that feeds the probiotic). A synbiotic may yield a synergistic interaction: its effect is larger than the sum of the effects of the probiotic and prebiotic (Note 2). Nineteen articles ...

Tags: Reviewer, Too: Origin of Life, Reviewer, too: Complexity

entry.title: The Theory of the Last Universal Common Ancestor LUCA

author.name: origins of life"|"origin of life"|"emergence of ...

entry.content:
Authors: K Saalbach

Journal: 2026

Abstract: This working paper provides a brief introduction to the Last Universal Common Ancestor LUCA and the potential biosafety implications. LUCA is the hypothetical precursor of all modern cells, ie, the Archaea, the Prokaryotes and Eukaryotes (which ...

entry.title: ... bioavailability and gender-specific fermentation of an Opuntia ficus-indica extract combining cladodes and fruit skins with synergistic prebiotic and metabolic actions

entry.content:
Authors: LM Cerrato, F Iannuzzo, A Pastore, A Blainski... - Journal of Functional Foods, 2026

Abstract: Opuntia ficus-indica (OFI) is traditionally recognized for its antioxidant and health-promoting properties, yet evidence on the bioaccessibility, bioavailability, microbiota-related effects and mechanism of action of standardized OFI extracts remains scarce. Here ...

entry.title: Nucleic acid catalysts: From the origin of life to modern applications

author.name: origins of life"|"origin of life"|"emergence of ...

entry.content:
Authors: S Müller - Current Opinion in Chemical Biology, 2026

Abstract: Naturally occurring ribozymes have been discovered, characterized, and used for ribozyme engineering. Furthermore, the powerful method of in vitro evolution has enabled the development of numerous artificial catalysts that mediate a wide range ...

entry.title: Ultrasound-Assisted Extraction Modifies the Structural Characteristics and Enhances the Prebiotic Potential of Large Yellow Tea Polysaccharides

entry.content:

entry.title: Evaluation of the prebiotic and antioxidant potential of Cordia africana hydrocolloid through in vitro gastrointestinal digestion

author.name: origins of life"|"origin of life"|"emergence of ...

entry.content:
Authors: MIF de Araújo, A González, P Ferreira-Santos... - Food Bioscience, 2026

Abstract: Cordia africana fruit contains a mucilaginous pulp rich in hydrocolloid, providing a natural source of bioactive polyphenols with potential prebiotic and antioxidant properties, whose bioaccessibility and impact on human intestinal microbiota remain ...

entry.title: Searching for Life -As-We-Don't-Know-It: Mission-relevant Application of Assembly Theory for Exoplanet Life Detection
entry.content:
Authors: L Slocombe

Abstract: ... This started in 1965 with Lovelock and the search for life on Mars . In the 1990s, the discovery of the first exoplanets propelled astrobiology beyond the Solar System. From 2009, Kepler revealed an unexpected diversity of exoplanets and stellar ...

Tags: Reviewer, Too: Astrobiology, Reviewer, Too: Exoplanets
